content.text: The Part Where People Get Hurt
<em>
Combat Overview

Combat in Arcane Steel is fast and dangerous. Most fights resolve in 3-5 rounds. Weapons hit hard, going to 0 HP means a Scar — permanent damage that accumulates over a career. Smart contractors win fast or don't fight at all.
Action Points
Each turn, a contractor has 5 Action Points (AP) to spend.
Standard Actions

rows: Make one attack with an equipped weapon.
Move one range band. Can be split across the turn.
Healing stim, grenade, scrubber, tech device.
Any activated augment ability (Burn, Redirect, etc.).
Open a door, pull a lever, grab an object, flip a switch.
Grant +2 to an adjacent ally's next roll this turn.
+2 Evasion until your next turn. Can stack with cover.
Any skill use in combat (Medicine, Tech, Stealth, etc.).
Reload a weapon that requires it (GM calls which weapons need reloading).
Switch to a different equipped weapon. Sheathe one, draw another.
Get up from Prone. Removes Prone condition.
Chrome check (DC set by grappler's Chrome or ability). Success = free. Failure = still grappled, AP wasted.
Melee only. Chrome check vs target's Chrome or Edge (target's choice). Success = target is Grappled.
Melee only. Chrome check vs target's Grit. Success = push target 1 range band OR knock Prone (your choice).
Move a Grappled target 1 range band with you. Requires maintaining grapple.
Medicine check on an adjacent downed ally. Prevents next Death Roll.
Move into available cover in your current zone. Gain In Cover condition (+2 Evasion vs ranged from covered direction).

At the end of your turn, convert all remaining AP to Reaction Points (RP). These RP are available until the start of your next turn. Use them for ranged overwatch fire on targets moving through your line of sight.
Ranged weapons only. Designate a zone. Until your next turn, enemies in that zone are Suppressed (-2 accuracy, +1 AP to advance toward you). Consumes ammo.
Hold your turn. Jump back in later this round. Must act before round ends or forfeit turn. Cannot Delay on consecutive rounds. Only one character per side may Delay per round.

content.text: Quick Actions (0 AP — one of each per turn)
Quick Actions are free and don't cost AP. You can use one of each Quick Action per turn — not one total.

rows: Let go of something. Hits the ground at your feet.
A short sentence. Shout a warning, call a target, coordinate. Not a full conversation.
Toggle a passive augment or ability on/off.
Voluntarily hit the ground. -2 Evasion but +2 vs ranged attacks from Medium+ range (smaller target).

content.text: Combining Actions

You can mix and match. Swing a Heavy Axe (4 AP) and Move (1 AP). Stab with a Knife three times (3 AP) and use a healing stim (1 AP) and Move (1 AP). Fire a Sniper Rifle (5 AP) and do nothing else. The weapon's AP cost determines how much of your turn it consumes.

Light weapons attack fast but hit soft. Heavy weapons attack slow but devastate. Your spare AP after attacking is your tactical flexibility — movement, items, defense. A contractor who commits all 5 AP to attacks can't move or use items that turn. A contractor who saves AP for movement and items is more versatile but deals less damage.
Reaction Points (RP)

Reactions happen outside your turn. They use Reaction Points (RP) — a separate resource from AP. Every contractor starts with 2 RP per round. RP refreshes at the start of your turn. Unused RP does not carry over between rounds.

content.label: RP Sources
content.text:
Baseline (all characters): 2 RP/round
Fortress T1 — Sentinel Reflexes: +1 RP/round
Fortress T3 — Fortress Wall: +2 RP/round, OAs cost 1 less RP (min 1)
AP-to-RP Conversion: At end of turn, convert all remaining AP to RP
Opportunity Attacks

When an enemy moves out of your melee range (Close → Medium or further) without using a Disengage-type ability, you may spend RP to make an Opportunity Attack. OAs now use your equipped weapon's full stats — accuracy die + stat bonus, weapon damage dice. They cost RP equal to the weapon's AP cost.

Design intent: Light weapons can always threaten reactively. Medium weapons need minor RP investment. Heavy weapons require dedicated augment investment (Fortress tree). A Bulwark with a Great Maul threatening OAs is a character who built specifically for that fantasy.

OA triggers remain unchanged: Only triggers on movement away from you. Moving around you at the same range doesn't trigger it. If the target uses Disengage (Dancer T2), Quick Escape Wiring (Shadow T1), or any ability that says "without provoking opportunity attacks," no OA is triggered.
AP-to-RP Conversion (Overwatch)

At the end of your turn, you may convert all remaining AP to RP. These RP are available until the start of your next turn. You may fire on targets moving through your line of sight (cone-shaped area). Each shot costs RP equal to the weapon's AP cost.

rows: Burst Pistol (2 RP) — YES (up to 2 shots)

content.text: Design intent: Overwatch is now a natural decision — shoot now and lose reactive capability, or hold fire and threaten the lane. Snipers who commit their full turn to watching a corridor are rewarded. The math self-balances.

content.label: Suppression vs Overwatch
content.text: Suppression (3 AP) remains unchanged as dedicated area denial — affects an entire zone with debuffs but deals no damage. AP-to-RP conversion provides targeted reactive fire. They serve different tactical roles: Suppression is area control, converted RP is targeted threat.
Other Reactions (RP Cost)
Reactions aren't limited to Opportunity Attacks. Several abilities and augments grant additional reaction options. All compete for the same RP pool:

rows: Enemy uses tech ability in range
Enemy attacks the ally you're covering

content.label: Detailed Rules
content.text: For full rules on Grappling and Shoving, see Special Actions. For Cover, Flanking, and Range Bands, see Positioning & Movement. For the full attack resolution steps, see Attacks & Damage.
